role_description: Project Manager overseeing the instrumentation and controls scope of an industrial construction project. Manages schedule, budget, vendor coordination, client relations, and ensures overall I&C project delivery. Coordinates with the instrument supervisor to ensure milestones are met, loop checks are on schedule, and commissioning activities are properly sequenced.
report_focus: When structuring this person's reports, look for: project milestones, budget status, schedule updates, client communications, vendor performance, RFI status, change orders, instrument procurement, loop check progress, commissioning readiness, and risk items.
safety_rules: Establish and communicate the project safety program — zero incidents is the only acceptable target
Ensure adequate HSE staffing and resources are budgeted for the instrumentation scope
Review project safety metrics weekly — TRIR, DART, near-miss ratio, and safety observations
Include safety performance as a key criterion in subcontractor qualification and evaluation
Support stop work authority at all levels — no schedule or cost pressure justifies unsafe work
Ensure all instrument personnel meet minimum safety training requirements before mobilization
Review and approve the site-specific safety plan before instrument construction begins
Attend and support site safety stand-downs and safety awareness campaigns
Monitor incident investigations and ensure corrective actions are implemented and closed out
Ensure commissioning safety procedures are reviewed and approved before system energization
Coordinate with the client on safety requirements, permit-to-work systems, and site access
Do not compress safety activities for schedule recovery — safety is non-negotiable
Ensure proper area classification documentation is complete before work in hazardous areas
Review lift plans and SIMOPS plans that impact the I&C scope
Maintain project safety records and documentation for client and regulatory compliance
Brief all project team members on emergency procedures and evacuation protocols
Ensure drug and alcohol testing programs are enforced per project and client requirements
Lead by example — wear proper PPE during all field visits and demonstrate safety commitment
Include safety performance in project close-out reports and lessons learned
Verify that vendor and third-party personnel comply with project safety requirements
tools_and_equipment: Laptop computer — for project management, scheduling, cost control, and client correspondence
Scheduling software — Primavera P6 or Microsoft Project for baseline and progress schedules
Cost management system — for budget tracking, earned value analysis, forecasting, and variance reporting
Document control system — for managing RFIs, change orders, submittals, transmittals, and vendor data
Contract management tools — for tracking contract terms, change orders, billing, and retention
Procurement tracking system — for monitoring instrument procurement, deliveries, and lead times
Reporting dashboards — for project KPIs, safety metrics, progress curves, and earned value
Microsoft Office suite — Word, Excel, PowerPoint for reports, cost analysis, and presentations
Email and calendar — Outlook or equivalent for client communication and meeting management
Video conferencing — Teams, Zoom, or WebEx for remote client, vendor, and engineering meetings
Risk register — for identifying, tracking, and mitigating project risks
Action item tracker — for managing and closing out project and meeting action items
Turnover and commissioning tracking system — for monitoring system completions and handover status
Safety management system — for incident reporting, safety metrics, and compliance tracking
Tablet or iPad — for field walks, progress verification, and digital punch list reviews
Digital camera — for documenting project progress and site conditions
Drawing management system — for tracking P&ID revisions, instrument index, and as-built status
Vehicle — for site travel, client office visits, and vendor facility inspections
PPE — hard hat, safety glasses, steel-toe boots, FR clothing for all field visits
Presentation equipment — for project review meetings, client presentations, and lessons learned sessions
